knot_assessments.notes: Likely double-wrapped surgeon's knot; partial occlusion during loop formation.
Hand switch preserved; extra regrasping before flattening.
Alternation back to left instrument; final stack stable.

technique_summary: Completed task without avulsion or gap; final knot stack stable and slit well-approximated. Time loss is dominated by the second throw (44s) and especially suture cutting (49s) — together ~93s, roughly half the run. Needle handling and placement were controlled. Performance is quality-preserving but inefficient: knot construction and tail trimming both need tightening.
improvement_suggestions: Present needle perpendicular before first bite to cut placement reorientation time.
Keep working loops smaller during throws 2 and 3 to reduce regrasping.
Adopt a fixed cutting routine: stabilize knot, isolate tail, cut, repeat — target 10-15s total.
